data.narrative: The student demonstrated excellent engineering judgment and debugging skills. They cleanly set up the database layer, correctly explained the compound index ordering on ClickEvent, and resolved the configuration failure immediately.

dimension_scores.observation: Explained composite index ordering column priority matching time-series query patterns.
dimension_scores.evidence: Filter by link_id first because we only want click events for one link, then sort/filter by clicked_at next.

dimension_scores.observation: Diagnosed Python interpreter mismatch and successfully isolated the commented-out env variable.
dimension_scores.evidence: I inspected the .env file and found that the DATABASE_URL line is currently commented out.

dimension_scores.observation: Configured Cascade deletes and hashed IP addresses to protect user privacy.
dimension_scores.evidence: Configured Cascade delete on ClickEvent and ip_hash mapping column.

dimension_scores.observation: Articulated how fail-fast validation at startup prevents silent runtime errors under traffic.
dimension_scores.evidence: Validating settings early prevents the application from accepting traffic while being unable to connect to critical services.

dimension_scores.observation: Exposed debugging hypotheses and conclusions clearly and concisely.
dimension_scores.evidence: My hypothesis is that the application is either not loading the correct .env file...

strengths: Hypothesis-driven debugging that isolated environment configuration errors quickly.
Clear explanation of left-to-right composite index ordering constraint.
data.recommendation: Continue focusing on fail-fast validation principles as we build out validation schemas and API endpoints in Module 3.

public_evidence.student_action: Implemented SQLAlchemy Link and ClickEvent schemas, defined time-series composite index, and set up Alembic migrations.
public_evidence.technical_detail: Link/ClickEvent schemas in models.py, Alembic migrations, database session lifecycle.
public_evidence.proof: Alembic migrations completed successfully and module-02-seed-query.py verified correct model insertion and query lookups.
public_evidence.ability: Design and verify validated persistence layers
public_evidence.reviewer_value: Proves ability to design an indexed relational schema and set up a validated configuration structure.

concepts_demonstrated.evidence: Set up Postgres models and database session lifecycle.
Identified commented-out database connection string in .env.
Articulated fail-fast benefits for server startup configuration.
Added and validated database_url config using Pydantic Settings.

teaching_approaches.detail: Used book index analogy to explain database lookup behavior.
